question.title: Find Smallest Common Element in All Rows
question.content:
Given an m x n matrix mat where every row is sorted in strictly increasing order, return the smallest common element in all rows.

If there is no common element, return -1.

 

Example 1:

Input: mat = [[1,2,3,4,5],[2,4,5,8,10],[3,5,7,9,11],[1,3,5,7,9]]
Output: 5

Example 2:

Input: mat = [[1,2,3],[2,3,4],[2,3,5]]
Output: 2

 

Constraints:

  • m == mat.length
  • n == mat[i].length
  • 1 <= m, n <= 500
  • 1 <= mat[i][j] <= 104
  • mat[i] is sorted in strictly increasing order.

hints: Notice that each row has no duplicates.
Is counting the frequency of elements enough to find the answer?
Use a data structure to count the frequency of elements.
Find an element whose frequency equals the number of rows.
